metrics.title: FY2026 Revenue Guidance
metrics.label: Non-China AI Capex Absorbs China Decline

moat.description: The only company in the world capable of producing EUV and High-NA EUV machines required for advanced chips below 7nm.
moat.analysisSummary: ASML is the **Sole Provider** of the world's most complex machines:

analysisPoints.title: EUV and High-NA Monopoly
analysisPoints.text: Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) lithography is required for every advanced chip from Apple, NVIDIA, and AMD. High-NA EUV — at $380M per system — extends this monopoly to 1.4nm and below, the only viable path to future AI silicon. ASML is the only company that can build either.

analysisPoints.title: Technological Barrier
analysisPoints.text: Developing EUV took 20+ years and billions in funding from TSMC, Intel, and Samsung co-investment. A competitor would need at minimum a decade and the cooperation of the entire semiconductor ecosystem to come close — and still wouldn't have ASML's 30+ years of machine performance data.

analysisPoints.title: Service Ecosystem and AI Demand Flywheel
analysisPoints.text: Once a machine is installed, ASML generates recurring service revenue for 20+ years. AI-driven chip demand is accelerating the pace of new installations: TSMC announced $54B in capex for 2026, SK Hynix alone is taking 12 EUV machines, and Q4 2025 bookings hit a record €13.2B — more than double analyst expectations.

growth.description: FY2025 revenue of €32.7B confirmed, beating initial guidance. FY2026 guided €34–39B as AI-driven capex from TSMC, Samsung, and SK Hynix absorbs the China revenue decline from expanded export controls. High-NA EUV shipments ramping into 2026 drive EPS growth faster than revenue via ASP and margin expansion. Company 2030 target of €44–60B implies a 10–13% revenue CAGR with meaningful operating leverage.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base: 75 (secular semiconductor capex compounder with EUV monopoly) +6 (AI infrastructure spend accelerating EUV adoption cycle; High-NA EUV adds premium ASP tier) -1 (China export controls create a structural revenue headwind ~10–15% of prior mix) = 80

drivers.name: High-NA EUV Mix
drivers.metric: First commercial units shipping; ASP ~$380M vs. ~$185M EUV

drivers.name: Service Revenue
drivers.metric: ~€6B+ recurring, ~18% of total revenue, 20-year contract base

drivers.name: Non-China Capex
drivers.metric: Taiwan +175% YoY, South Korea +46%, Japan +50% in 2025

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: US/Dutch export controls expand to cover remaining DUV immersion tools currently still legal, eliminating remaining China revenue (~10% of total) simultaneously with a hyperscaler AI capex pause in 2026–2027

valuation.description: At ~$870 — now near and briefly below the bear case ($850) — ASML is at one of the most attractive entry points since the 2022 semiconductor correction. The broad market correction (tariff uncertainty, macro slowdown fears) has created a price that implies the bear scenario is almost fully priced in, despite FY2026 guidance remaining intact. Forward P/E near 25× is a meaningful discount to the 5-year average of ~40×.

bear.description: Expanded DUV/EUV export restrictions to China coincide with a broad AI capex slowdown, causing a semiconductor cycle downturn and multiple compression toward 20–22×.
points: US/Dutch export controls extend to remaining DUV immersion tools, eliminating the last ~10% of China revenue in a single regulatory action
AI infrastructure spending stalls in 2026–2027 as hyperscalers digest overbuilt capacity, trimming TSMC and memory capex plans simultaneously
High-NA EUV margin dilution persists longer than expected, compressing gross margin toward 50% and triggering EPS estimate reductions across the Street

base.description: High-NA EUV rollout continues on schedule, non-China capex absorbs China decline, FY2026 revenue of €36–38B is achieved, and margin expansion toward 55%+ re-rates the stock.
points: Non-China revenue (Taiwan, South Korea, Japan, US CHIPS Act fabs) fully offsets China decline; FY2026 revenue lands in the €36–37B range
High-NA EUV scales to 20+ annual shipments, driving mix shift toward higher-ASP tools and progressive gross margin improvement toward 55%
AI-driven HBM and advanced logic demand sustains TSMC/SK Hynix capex growth through 2027, extending the EUV order cycle

bull.description: AI-driven data centre demand accelerates EUV adoption through 2028, ASML reaches €50B+ revenue ahead of 2030 guidance, and High-NA ramp drives a significant earnings re-rating.
points: Sovereign foundry programmes (US CHIPS Act, EU Chips Act, Japan, Middle East domestic fabs) add a new demand cohort; ASML reaches €50B revenue by 2028
High-NA EUV reaches 30+ shipments annually by 2027 at improving margins; gross margin hits 56%+ ahead of 2030 target, compressing P/E multiples to historical discount
AI accelerator demand for 1.4nm and 1nm nodes materialises faster than consensus, doubling ASML's addressable EUV shipment window

learnedInterfaces.note: Not applicable — ASML sells precision lithography hardware to chip fabricators, not UI-based software.

businessLogic.note: Not applicable to extreme ultraviolet lithography physics and optics manufacturing.

publicDataAccess.note: Not applicable to this hardware monopoly business model.

talentScarcity.note: EUV optics engineers, plasma physicists, and precision mechatronic specialists are among the rarest people on Earth. AI cannot replicate 30 years of applied physics expertise embedded in ASML's workforce.

bundling.note: Full EUV system bundle: laser light source + optics + scanner + metrology + software + 20-year service contract = inseparable, multi-year integration. High-NA EUV adds a new, higher-ASP bundle tier no competitor can match.

proprietaryData.note: 30+ years of machine performance data from every major chip fab run generates yield-optimisation intelligence that is compounding, exclusive, and the foundation of the recurring service contract moat.

regulatoryLockIn.note: Dutch export-controlled technology requires government approval per sale; classified as dual-use under Wassenaar. No Chinese fab can receive EUV systems. TSMC/Samsung/Intel dependency creates a state-protected monopoly with no legislative path to disruption.

networkEffects.note: Deep co-development IP partnerships with TSMC, Samsung, and Intel create mutual dependency. AI chip demand is widening this loop — more compute demand drives more fab investment which drives more ASML machines. Not yet 'strong' as it remains bilateral rather than self-reinforcing at scale.

transactionEmbedding.note: ASML EUV machines run 24/7 inside fabs generating chips the world runs on. Removing them mid-cycle requires halting production lines worth billions per day. AI server demand makes these machines more mission-critical, not less.

systemOfRecord.note: ASML IS the system of record for advanced node semiconductor manufacturing globally. High-NA EUV is the only viable path to 1.4nm and below, locking in ASML's monopoly position through at least 2035.

tenMoats.verdict: ASML is the most AI-resilient business in the portfolio. Physical hardware monopolies, regulatory protection, and manufacturing expertise rooted in physics cannot be disrupted by software AI — and the AI infrastructure boom is actively accelerating demand for ASML's machines. High-NA EUV cements this monopoly for the next decade.
